home / blog / top

how my website changed my life

when i first started working on this website, i was simply just bored out of my mind. i didn't have any big plans for my website. i wasn't studying or working at the time. during that time it was just me and my computer against the world. who would have thought this very very silly project that i started exactly 3 years ago would turn out to be such a massive positive influence in my life?

during the first year of working on my website, i was really struggling to find any meaning to my life. i didn't know what i wanted to do and i had spent years already in this limbo where if someone asked me what i wanted to do with my life i would just say "i don't know". my biggest problem was the i knew all of this. i knew that i eventually had to do something. anything. but i still didn't know what the hell i wanted. i felt paralyzed. i wish i could just say my plan was just to "chill and have a nice time" but that's not the kind of world i (we?) live in. i knew that i had to do something about this eventually.

besides my struggles of finding something to do, i also felt very alone. i barely had any friends. the only people i would frequently talk to were my grandma and my girlfriend, which were an immense source of love and support for me at the time.

when i got into this hobby i was just some guy around the internet. but i loved it. i love the feeling of learning something new and being able to create something with it. i love customizing stuff to my liking. i love having my own space. the truth is that this website was an escape of all of these feelings i was going through at the time. it felt nice to create something and have that resonate with people during a time where i felt really useless. i had nothing else to do, so i would spend hours and hours creating every page in here. starting out using HTML templates, and then within a month i was able to make these pages from scratch. people write things on my guestbook such as i can't imagine how much it took to build this website and they are so right. i would say about 90% of my website is the product of working on this on hours to end for 10 months straight. and i was enjoying it all. but as the days passed, i still had that question in my mind: what am i gonna do?. and as the months passed i felt more and more anxious. i needed to do something about my future. anything.

one night i sat down on my desk and started writing on a piece of paper the following:

employment era ideas

  1. work on my art? - i dont think my drawing skills would have ever taking me anywhere.
  2. translation jobs / remote jobs - i don't have reliable internet or power for this to work.
  3. retail - i applied to many and none of the had gotten back to. regardless, i think death is a better option than working in retail my entire life.
i need to stop being so scared.

studying ideas: something related to web development or something that can lead me there?

and so it began.

i went on a full on research of things i could do that were someone related to this new pass time of mine. and i know very well what they say about making your hobby your job, you get burnt out and all of that. i understand that very well. but that was the entire point of all of this. the root of my problems. i wasn't passionate on anything enough to give a fuck or to be motivated to pursue it. but this? i really do give a fuck about this and i loved learning about it too. i really saw it as my very last try to do something with my life. whatever i did, it needed to have a purpose. which is why i decided to go with something related to the hobby i already had and loved.

many things showed up during my research: web designer, UX designer, product design, programming, and eventually i reached the very end of it. a computer science degree could help me out get there. somewhere! anywhere... it was very closely related to my hobby. the idea of studying computer science really intrigued me lots. but i also have to admit that it was a choice i made out of desperation to do anything. i vividly remember crying as i wrote my previous notes on the paper. feeling like it was the last chance i had. but at least this time i had a purpose as to why i would pursue such a degree. these negative feelings i had where mostly because i was scared. scared of trying and ending up nowhere. of failing. again: i need to stop being so scared.

going back to university seemed insane to me. specially for such a degree that is so heavy on math. i'll say it a billion times. i am not good at math. people keep saying i'm good at it because i get good grades and i kinda enjoy the process of doing a good ass math exercise. but i am not good with numbers. what i am good at is studying. i'm very slow with numbers. if you ask me what's 11 - 8 i genuinely need a moment so i can count back with my fingers. and yet i didn't care. i couldn't care! i had to give this a chance. my chance to do anything. i kept on my research looking at my public universities and their programs and requisites, until one day i decided to call one day to ask for an orientation around september 2024.

passed through every quest my university hit me with during the next 3 months and eventually started my first semester in january 2024. sometimes, the timing of things in life can seem a bit insane. a few days before starting my semester, the person who i was sharing a household with for the past 2 years had just died: my grandma. my mom had flown all the way to Puerto Rico once i told her that my grandma, her mother, was in the hospital. me and my mom were basically the only ones there throughout the situation with my grandma. let me tell you, emptying a 3 bedroom house on your own is not fun. being caught up all of this, i didn't have any time to do or even think of the paperwork left for me to do or in what classes i was supposed to enroll with or even how i was supposed to do that.

on the last day of my mom's visit and a day before classes started, she offered to come with me to campus. i'm pretty sure she was worried for me. worried that i would go into a depressive state because of my grandma's death and not follow through my newly goal of studying. in reality i sort of had mixed feelings. i remember that same day she squeezed me really tight while walking around campus and she told me that she was really happy for me that i would start studying again. specially in that campus. the very first university in Puerto Rico. this place is very special, she said.

when my classes started i had very mixed feelings. i found myself overjoyed to be studying once again, but i was also grieving my grandma. though i can say most of the time i was very happy to be where i was. nowadays i still get a little sad that i am not able to talk to my grandma about my journey in university once again.

i really love going to university. i love studying, taking the train, and going to my classes. i have been called the happiest student on campus. i was doing really good in my classes. but i mostly really enjoy the community. i've always been pretty shy and introverted, so making friends was a bit hard for me during these early days of studying. despite that, i still found myself talking to people and hanging out with them. did those friendship last? not really. but i'm not mad about that or anything. people come and go.

my 2nd semester in my university was a very special one. i started taking my first computer science class this semester. let me tell you, the smile i had on my face to be able to be in that class was kind of embarrassing. i was SO happy to be there even the professor noticed. it always sounds so silly to say that me fucking around with HTML and CSS led me to computer science. it makes me feel a little bit childish in a sense. but maybe it's because what i do isn't very professional. my entire website is kind of just a blog with extra steps at the end of the day. but whatever!! i was having so much fun. i really liked whenever i would apply something learned in class to my website. i never knew doing boolean algebra could be so fun.

all of my professors (UP TO THAT POINT) had been so chill and nice to me. during halloween i dressed up as award strobe hello miku and went to my precalculus class just like that. when the professor walked in he said oh yeah it's halloween... that a pretty cool outfit. and then a few hours later i was at his office as miku asking him about some bullshit that i didn't understand from class. i really liked this professor. 70% of the professors i've met want to quit because of the misery they get paid for all the work they do. whenever i would go to his office he would have a tab in his browser with the lottery website. i asked him one day would you still give classes if you won the lottery? to which he replied you guys won't see me around if i do win. despite all of this, he was still more than happy to help me.

as the time passed i got to open myself a little bit more and more to people. i managed to get a little part time job at a retail store. not much goes on here except for the weekly $50~ i get from it nowadays. but i've gotten to meet some pretty nice people from it that have become my friends as well.

during my 2nd year of studying, i eventually find out of this organization made up of students in my computer science department that help out other students and give workshops and talks about useful stuff and tools to know that don't get a lot of love inside of our classes. i joined one of these workshops one day, this one about git. despite already using git for my website, i thought i could maybe learn a new thing or two about it. this was really funny because at the end of the day they noticed that i was actually... not new to the world of git. one of them visited my website and then came to talk to me because of it. he was amazed that my website was simply pure HTML and CSS done by me. i have this little interaction very near to my heart.

eventually, i got to join this little group. got to meet lots of people and befriend a couple of them as well. meeting these people has been such a highlight of my time in university. it almost feels like i haven't made connections like these in a while. i am always happy to be with them. it just makes me really happy to have such a range of people that i can talk to and that have similar interests to me after feeling so lonely for so long.

maybe saying that i am where i am because of my website is a bit much. but i don't think my life would be the same if i hadn't created an account on neocities one day. even if i am fighting for my life right now trying to pass my computer architecture class. even if i am oh so well aware that a bachelor's degree in computer science is practically useless in the job market right now. despite everything, i am still way happier today than i was 3 years ago. and i still enjoy learning and doing what i do which is the most important part of it all. i've even been commissioned to design and make sites for other people! where would i be if i had never found this hobby of mine? i don't want to know... but what i do know is that all those hours i've spent on my website have led me here. and that is good enough for me.

written with love by des, 11.apr.26
